title: Schedule coordinator lifecycle event for uniform recovery
purpose: The schedule coordinator workflow has no WorkflowStarted event in the store, requiring a special-case skip in repopulate_active_workflows and a separate catchup_schedule_coordinator method. This is fragile — any code path that iterates active workflows must know about the exception. Give the coordinator a proper WorkflowStarted event so recovery handles it uniformly with all other workflows.
task: Record a WorkflowStarted event for the schedule coordinator during EngineBuilder::build() when the coordinator has no existing history. On subsequent builds against the same store, the coordinator already has history and the Recorder catches up normally (via catchup_schedule_coordinator). Remove the special-case skip in repopulate_active_workflows and ensure the coordinator is recovered through the standard active-workflow path.

requirements.title: Record coordinator WorkflowStarted on first engine build
requirements.spec: WHEN EngineBuilder::build() creates a schedule coordinator against a store with no coordinator history, THE SYSTEM SHALL record a WorkflowStarted event for the coordinator workflow_id with workflow_type 'aion.schedule_coordinator' and empty input. WHEN the store already has coordinator history, THE SYSTEM SHALL skip the initial event and use catchup_schedule_coordinator to resume at the correct head.
acceptance: First engine build against an empty store records one WorkflowStarted event for the coordinator.
Second engine build against the same store does not record a duplicate WorkflowStarted.
The coordinator appears in store.list_active() after the first build.

requirements.title: Remove special-case skip in repopulate_active_workflows
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL remove the schedule_coordinator skip in repopulate_active_workflows. The coordinator SHALL be recovered through the standard ActiveWorkflowRecoverySeam like any other workflow. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a coordinator-specific recovery implementation that re-arms the schedule evaluator rather than spawning a beamr process.
acceptance: repopulate_active_workflows no longer contains a skip for schedule_coordinator_workflow_id().
The coordinator is recovered via the standard recovery seam path.
After recovery, schedule_evaluator is re-armed and can fire timers.
The DeferredActiveWorkflowRecovery (used in tests without schedule infrastructure) handles the coordinator gracefully (skips or provides a no-op recovery).

requirements.title: Schedule lifecycle test passes without special-case handling
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL verify that the schedule lifecycle integration test continues to pass with the coordinator recovered through the standard path. The TestRecovery seam from AX-006 SHALL handle the coordinator as an active workflow without special casing.
acceptance: The TestRecovery struct does not special-case the coordinator workflow_id.
After engine restart, list_schedules returns the correct schedule state.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the schedule evaluator or coordinator's runtime behaviour — only its initialization and recovery path.
SHALL NOT remove catchup_schedule_coordinator — it is still needed for the case where the coordinator has existing history.
SHALL NOT spawn a beamr process for the coordinator — it is a virtual workflow that only holds schedule events.
SHALL NOT change the coordinator's deterministic workflow_id.
verification: The schedule_coordinator skip no longer exists in builder.rs.
store.list_active() includes the coordinator after first engine build.
